{{Quote|Leave that to me. Send a distress signal. Then inform the senate that all onboard were killed.|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|<ref name="Episode 4">{{Film|IV}}</ref>}}
The '''''Wide-Eyed''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] survey vessel that claimed to have received a text-based distress signal from the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']]. Though the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] ship had, in actuality, been [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|captured]] by the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']], the [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|BoSS]] report filed by ''Wide-Eyed'' stated otherwise. The fabricated distress signal read:

:"PRIORITY DISTRESS ALERT: Captain [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|R. Antilles]], ''Tantive IV''; [[House of Organa/Legends|House of Alderaan]] consular vessel. We have suffered catastrophic depressurization and hull damage as a result of [[Lightspeed/Legends|superluminal]] collision with uncharted asteroidal body. Area is dense with debris and hazardous, but we require immediate assistance and medical attention. Advise strict anti-rad protocols due to nearby stellar conditions."

In reality, the distress signal had been sent by 501st Legion commander [[Daine Jir/Legends|Daine Jir]] under the orders of Darth Vader to cover up the ''Devastator''{{'s}} capture of the vessel, with the resulting report from the ''Wide-Eyed'' being informed falsely that the ''Tantive IV'' had hit an asteroid with no survivors.

Roughly sixteen standard hours later, the ''Wide-Eyed'' "arrived" on scene and reported visual confirmation of the ''Tantive IV''{{'s}} demise and [[Life form indicator|sensor]] confirmation that all hands were dead, including then-[[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. About [[9 ABY/Legends|nine years later]], during the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] rule, the BoSS casefile was closed and the fabricated story exposed.
